sections.paragraph:
has a double power; the one proper, the other derived. The proper is-

(1) to be curved, hollow, like an arch or vault, whence גַּב something gibbous; this sense is widely extended in the kindred roots, as גָּוַו, גָּוַה, whence גַּו, גֵּו; גָּפַף, whence גַּף; גּוּף, whence גּוּפָה; also כָּפַף (where see more) and כָּפָה; קָבַב, قاب, and also חָקַף. To this answer gibbus, Giebel, Gipfel. Also the roots גָּבַהּ, גָּבַח, גָּבַן, גָּבַע.

(2) The other is borrowed from גּוּב, יָגַב to cut, to dig; Arab. جَبَّ to cut, to cut out; comp. جَبَ Med. Waw and Ye id. and Conj. VIII. to dig a well. Whence Ch. גֹּב a well.
